title: Browser Use
description: Make websites accessible for AI agents. The easiest way to connect your AI agents with the browser.
author: browser-use
content:
# Browser Use

Make websites accessible for AI agents.

## Quick Start

```bash
pip install browser-use
playwright install
```

## Features
- 🌐 Browser Control - AI agents can control real browsers
- 📸 Visual Understanding - See and interact with pages visually
- 🔍 Smart Element Detection - Automatically find interactive elements
- 📝 Natural Language Actions - Control browser with natural language
- ♾️ Multiple LLM Support - OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, local models

## Installation
```python
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from browser_use import Agent
import asyncio

llm = ChatOpenAI(model='gpt-4o')
agent = Agent(
    task='Find the cheapest flight from NYC to LA',
    llm=llm
)
asyncio.run(agent.run())
```

**Website**: https://browser-use.com
**GitHub**: https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use (89k+ stars)

title: SmolaAgents
description: The most affordable and reliable agent framework by Hugging Face. Build agents with minimal code.
author: huggingface
content:
# SmolaAgents

A framework for building agents that write Python code to solve tasks.

## Quick Start
```bash
pip install smolagents
```

## Core Features
- 🦙 Lightweight - Minimal dependencies
- 🛠️ Code-First - Agents write Python code to solve tasks
- 🤝 Multi-Modal - Support for vision, browsing, and tool use
- 🔄 ReAct Pattern - Thought → Action → Observation loops
- 🔒 Sandboxed Execution - Safe code execution environment

## Example
```python
from smolagents import CodeAgent, DuckDuckGoSearchTool

agent = CodeAgent(
    tools=[DuckDuckGoSearchTool()],
    model=your_model
)
agent.run("What's the current weather in Tokyo?")
```

**Docs**: https://huggingface.co/docs/smolagents
**GitHub**: https://github.com/huggingface/smolagents (26k stars)

title: CrewAI
description: Framework for orchestrating role-playing, autonomous AI agents. Build multi-agent systems with ease.
author: crewAIInc
content:
# CrewAI

Framework for orchestrating role-playing, autonomous AI agents.

## Quick Start
```bash
pip install crewai
crewai create crew my_project
```

## Core Concepts
- 👥 Crews - Teams of agents working together
- 🎭 Agents - Role-playing AI workers with specific goals
- 📋 Tasks - Units of work assigned to agents
- 🔄 Processes - Sequential or hierarchical workflows
- 🛠️ Tools - Capabilities agents can use

## Example
```python
from crewai import Agent, Crew, Task

researcher = Agent(role='Researcher', goal='Find information')
writer = Agent(role='Writer', goal='Create content')

crew = Crew(agents=[researcher, writer], tasks=[task1, task2])
result = crew.kickoff()
```

**Website**: https://crewai.com
**GitHub**: https://github.com/crewAIInc/crewAI (49k stars)

title: Crawlee (Python)
description: Web scraping and browser automation library for Python. Build reliable crawlers fast.
author: apify
content:
# Crawlee for Python

Web scraping and browser automation library.

## Installation
```bash
pip install crawlee
```

## Features
- ⚡ Fast Crawling - Parallel processing with asyncio
- 🧠 Smart Queuing - Automatic URL deduplication and retry
- 🔒 Anti-Bot Protection - Proxy rotation and fingerprint rotation
- 📸 Browser Integration - Built-in Playwright support
- 📦 Storage - Automatic dataset and key-value store

## Example
```python
from crawlee.playwright import PlaywrightCrawler
from crawlee.router import Router

crawler = PlaywrightCrawler()

@crawler.router.default_handler
async def handler(context):
    data = {
        'title': await context.page.title(),
        'url': context.request.url,
    }
    await context.push_data(data)

await crawler.run(['https://example.com'])
```

**Docs**: https://crawlee.dev
**GitHub**: https://github.com/apify/crawlee-python (22k stars)

title: MarkItDown (Microsoft)
description: Convert various file formats (PDF, Word, Excel, PowerPoint, etc.) to Markdown. By Microsoft.
author: microsoft
content:
# MarkItDown

Convert various file formats to Markdown.

## Quick Start
```bash
pip install markitdown
```

## Supported Formats
- 📄 PDF documents (.pdf)
- 📊 Word documents (.docx, .doc)
- 📈 Excel spreadsheets (.xlsx, .xls)
- 📽️ PowerPoint presentations (.pptx, .ppt)
- 🖼️ Images (.png, .jpg, .jpeg) with OCR
- 🎵 Audio files (.mp3, .wav) with transcription
- 📁 ZIP archives
- 🔗 Web pages (via URL)

## Example
```python
from markitdown import MarkItDown

md = MarkItDown()
result = md.convert('document.pdf')
print(result.text_content)
```

**GitHub**: https://github.com/microsoft/markitdown (36k stars)
